summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Python, Linux Consumption) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI for the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists results to an Azure Storage Blob container. Authentication is exclusively via Managed Identity (no API keys). Infrastructure is provisioned via Bicep; CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
assumptions: Python 3.11 on Azure Functions Linux Consumption plan is the target runtime.
TRAPI endpoint is accessible from the Function App's managed identity with appropriate role assignments.
Blob container name is 'yankees-roster' as established in existing infra conventions.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) — no storage connection strings.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD platform.
TRAPI refers to the internal GPT-4o proxy/gateway; its base URL and scope will be provided via app settings.
No VNet integration is required unless specified later.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and OAuth scope required for GPT-4o access?
What Managed Identity role is required to call TRAPI — is it an Entra app role or Azure RBAC?
What is the exact blob output format — JSON array, CSV, newline-delimited?
Should each nightly run overwrite a single blob or append/version per run?
What Azure subscription and resource group should be targeted for deployment?
Are there specific performance SLAs (e.g., function must complete within N minutes)?
Is there a cost budget constraint that affects the hosting plan choice?
What alerting channel should be used (email, Teams, PagerDuty)?
Should the function emit any downstream events after writing (e.g., queue message)?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish provisioned infrastructure, identity model, CI/CD pipeline, and development environment so all subsequent build tasks have a deployable target.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group available.
TRAPI endpoint URL and scope confirmed.
GitHub repository access confirmed for CI/CD setup.
exit_criteria: Bicep templates deploy successfully to target environment.
Managed Identity created and RBAC roles assigned.
GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs on PR with lint and unit test steps.
Function App reachable and AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName resolves correctly.

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the TRAPI client, blob writer module, and the nightly timer-triggered Azure Function that orchestrates the end-to-end roster fetch and store flow.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint accessible from Function App identity.
Blob container 'yankees-roster' provisioned and accessible via Managed Identity.
Local dev environment configured with Python 3.11 and dependencies installed.
exit_criteria: TRAPI client returns a non-empty 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o.
Blob writer successfully writes roster data to 'yankees-roster' container.
Timer function triggers on schedule and completes end-to-end flow.
All unit tests passing with coverage reported.

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add error handling, retries, idempotency, observability, and security controls to make the function production-reliable.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met.
Function deployed to Azure and timer trigger confirmed firing.
exit_criteria: Function retries on transient TRAPI/storage failures without duplicate writes.
Structured logs emitted to Application Insights on success and failure.
Alert rule fires on function failure.
Security scan confirms zero API keys or secrets in code or app settings.
All tests passing including failure-scenario unit tests.

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in production, confirm monitoring is active, and complete documentation for handoff.
exit_criteria: Function executes successfully in production on nightly schedule.
Roster blob present and readable in 'yankees-roster' container after first run.
All success criteria in spec.md checked off.

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan for completeness, correctness, and alignment with spec.md requirements. Confirm open questions are tracked and assumptions are acceptable before implementation begins.
outputs: Confirmed assumptions and open questions log
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by rciapala or designated tech lead.
Open questions triaged and assigned owners.
Assumptions validated or updated.
Plan approved and Foundation wave unblocked.
risks: Unresolved TRAPI endpoint details block INF-03 and APP-01.

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep (Storage, Function App, RBAC)
tasks.description: Author and deploy Bicep modules for Azure Storage Account (with 'yankees-roster' blob container), Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11), System-Assigned Managed Identity, and RBAC role assignments (Storage Blob Data Contributor). Use identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName; no storage connection strings. Validate all resources deploy cleanly via `az deployment group create`.
inputs: Existing infra/ Bicep conventions from repo memory
Target Azure subscription and resource group
acceptance_criteria: Bicep templates pass `az bicep build` with no errors.
`az deployment group create` succeeds with no error codes.
Storage Account and 'yankees-roster' blob container exist in Azure portal.
Function App created with Python 3.11 Linux Consumption plan.
System-Assigned Managed Identity enabled on Function App.
Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned to Function App identity on storage account.
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting present; no AzureWebJobsStorage connection string.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
Infrastructure validated by running `az functionapp show` and `az storage container show --name yankees-roster`.
risks: Subscription-level RBAC permissions may be required to assign roles — confirm with Azure admin.
Consumption plan cold-start latency may affect nightly timer reliability.

tasks.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for build, test, and deploy
tasks.description: Create GitHub Actions workflows: (1) CI workflow on PR — install Python 3.11 deps, run pytest with coverage, lint with flake8; (2) CD workflow on merge to main — deploy Function App via `az functionapp deployment source config-zip` or Azure Functions Action. Use OIDC federated identity for Azure authentication (no service principal secrets).
inputs: Function App name from INF-01 outputs
outputs: OIDC federated credential configured in Azure
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow triggers on pull_request and runs lint + pytest successfully.
CD workflow triggers on push to main and deploys to Function App without errors.
Azure authentication uses OIDC (no secrets stored in GitHub).
GitHub Actions secrets contain no API keys or storage connection strings.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
Deployment verified by checking Function App version in Azure portal after CD run.
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires Azure AD app registration — confirm permissions.
Function App deployment slot strategy not defined; assume direct production deploy.

tasks.title: Validate TRAPI endpoint access from Function App Managed Identity
tasks.description: Confirm the Function App's System-Assigned Managed Identity can authenticate to the TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint. Configure required app settings (TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_SCOPE) as Function App application settings. Document the token acquisition flow (azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential + get_token). Validate with a manual test invocation or az rest call.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and OAuth scope (from open question resolution)
outputs: TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_SCOPE app settings configured in Function App
Validated token acquisition from Managed Identity
Connection test evidence (logs or curl output)
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_SCOPE app settings present in Function App configuration (no hardcoded values).
Manual token acquisition from Managed Identity succeeds (HTTP 200 from token endpoint).
Test call to TRAPI endpoint returns a valid response (HTTP 200) using the Managed Identity token.
No API keys or client secrets used anywhere in the auth flow.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
Evidence of successful TRAPI connection captured in Application Insights or az cli output.
risks: TRAPI may require explicit role assignment or app registration consent for the Managed Identity — this is a critical blocker.
TRAPI may not support Managed Identity auth; fallback strategy undefined.

tasks.title: Enforce no-API-key security policy across all configuration
tasks.description: Audit all app settings, code, and CI/CD configuration to ensure zero API keys or connection strings are used. Enforce Managed Identity for all Azure service access (Storage, TRAPI). Add a pre-commit or CI check (e.g., truffleHog or gitleaks) to detect accidental secret commits.
inputs: spec.md Security NFR: No API Keys
outputs: CI secret-scanning step added to GitHub Actions workflow
Confirmed zero secrets in codebase and app settings
acceptance_criteria: gitleaks or truffleHog scan runs in CI and returns zero findings.
No AzureWebJobsStorage connection string, TRAPI API key, or storage account key present in app settings, code, or GitHub secrets.
All Azure service connections use DefaultAzureCredential / Managed Identity.
Security review sign-off documented in PR.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
risks: Developer tooling or local .env files may inadvertently introduce secrets into git history.

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client module to fetch 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o
tasks.description: Implement a Python module `trapi_client.py` that acquires an OAuth token via azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential, calls the TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint with a prompt to list all 1985 New York Yankees roster members, and returns a structured list of player names. Include retry logic for transient HTTP errors. Write unit tests using pytest with mocked HTTP responses; run with `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=trapi_client --cov-report=term-missing`.
inputs: INF-03 validated TRAPI endpoint and scope
azure-identity, httpx or requests Python packages
acceptance_criteria: trapi_client.py returns a non-empty list of 1985 Yankees player names when called against live TRAPI endpoint.
Unit tests cover: successful response parsing, empty response handling, HTTP 429 retry, HTTP 500 error propagation.
All pytest tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v --cov=trapi_client` with ≥80% coverage.
Test results link included in PR description.
No API keys or hardcoded credentials in module or tests.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
risks: GPT-4o response format may be inconsistent — prompt engineering needed to ensure parseable structured output.
TRAPI rate limits may affect retries under load.

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob writer module to store roster data
tasks.description: Implement a Python module `blob_writer.py` that uses azure-storage-blob with DefaultAzureCredential to write the player roster (as JSON) to the 'yankees-roster' blob container. Blob naming convention: `roster-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json` (one blob per run, overwrite if exists). Write unit tests with mocked BlobServiceClient; run with `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=blob_writer --cov-report=term-missing`.
inputs: INF-01 provisioned storage account and blob container
acceptance_criteria: blob_writer.py successfully writes a JSON blob to 'yankees-roster' container using Managed Identity (no connection string).
Blob named with date pattern `roster-YYYY-MM-DD.json` exists in container after write.
Unit tests cover: successful write, blob overwrite idempotency, storage exception propagation.
All pytest tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py -v --cov=blob_writer` with ≥80% coverage.
Test results link included in PR description.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
risks: Managed Identity RBAC assignment propagation delay may cause 403 errors in early testing.
Blob overwrite behavior must be confirmed — ensure no data loss on re-run.

tasks.title: Implement nightly timer-triggered Azure Function orchestrating TRAPI fetch and blob write
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function entry point `function_app.py` with a TimerTrigger set to CRON schedule `0 0 * * *` (nightly at 00:00 UTC). The function calls trapi_client to fetch the roster and blob_writer to persist it. Include structured logging (logging module) for start, success, and failure events. Write integration-style unit tests with mocked trapi_client and blob_writer; run with `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=function_app --cov-report=term-missing`.
acceptance_criteria: TimerTrigger CRON expression is `0 0 * * *` in function decorator.
Function successfully invokes trapi_client and blob_writer end-to-end in integration smoke test.
Structured log entries emitted for: function start, TRAPI call success/failure, blob write success/failure, function end.
Unit tests cover: successful execution, TRAPI failure path, blob write failure path.
All pytest tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/test_function_app.py -v --cov=function_app` with ≥80% coverage.
Test results link included in PR description.
local.settings.json is in .gitignore.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
risks: Timer trigger CRON timezone: Azure Functions timer is UTC by default — confirm UTC 00:00 is acceptable.
Cold-start on Consumption plan may add latency to first nightly execution.

tasks.title: Implement error handling, retries, and idempotency for nightly function
tasks.description: Enhance trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with configurable retry logic (exponential backoff) for transient failures. Ensure the function is idempotent: writing the same date's blob twice must not corrupt data. Add exception handling that logs structured error details and does not silently swallow failures. Add unit tests for all failure and retry scenarios.
outputs: Updated function_app.py with structured error handling
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff on HTTP 429 and 5xx responses.
Blob write is idempotent: running function twice on same date produces identical blob without error.
Unhandled exceptions are caught, logged with structured error detail, and re-raised to mark function execution as failed.
Unit tests cover: max retry exhaustion, idempotent write, structured error log output.
All pytest tests pass including new failure-scenario tests with ≥80% overall coverage.
Test results link included in PR description.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
risks: Retry storms under sustained TRAPI outage may exhaust function execution timeout (default 5 min on Consumption).
Idempotency relies on date-keyed blob naming — confirm date source is deterministic (UTC date).

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights logging, metrics, and alerting
tasks.description: Provision an Application Insights resource (via Bicep) and wire it to the Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting (no instrumentation key). Configure a metric alert rule that fires when the function has ≥1 failure in a 24-hour window. Verify structured logs from function_app.py appear in Application Insights Live Metrics and Log Analytics.
inputs: Azure Monitor / Application Insights Bicep module
outputs: Alert rule deployed in Azure Monitor
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights resource deployed via Bicep with no instrumentation key (connection string only).
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING present in Function App app settings.
Function execution logs visible in Application Insights 'traces' table within 5 minutes of invocation.
Alert rule configured: fires on ≥1 function failure in 24h window; notification channel defined.
Alert tested by triggering a deliberate function failure and confirming alert fires.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and approved before merge.
Infrastructure validated: `az monitor metrics alert show` returns the alert rule.
risks: Alert notification channel (email/Teams) not defined in spec — confirm with rciapala.
Application Insights sampling may suppress low-volume logs — configure sampling rate appropriately.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test and pre-production validation
tasks.description: Execute a full end-to-end validation: manually trigger the Azure Function in production/staging, verify the TRAPI call returns the complete 1985 Yankees roster, confirm the JSON blob is written to 'yankees-roster' with correct naming and content, and validate all Application Insights logs are present. Verify the nightly timer fires correctly by checking next scheduled run.
acceptance_criteria: Manual function trigger succeeds (HTTP 202 / execution status Success in portal).
Blob `roster-{today}.json` exists in 'yankees-roster' container and contains ≥20 player names.
Application Insights shows structured start, TRAPI success, blob write success, and end log entries.
No API keys or secrets found in any log output.
Timer trigger next scheduled execution visible in Function App monitor.
E2E test results documented and linked in PR or issue comment.
risks: GPT-4o roster completeness is non-deterministic — define acceptance threshold (e.g., ≥20 players) to validate.
First nightly timer run cannot be tested until 00:00 UTC — manual trigger used as proxy.

tasks.title: Production deployment, documentation, and handoff
tasks.description: Execute final production deployment via CD pipeline, confirm nightly schedule is active, update README with architecture overview, TRAPI dependency, blob output format, and runbook for alert response. Confirm all spec.md success criteria are checked off.
outputs: Production Function App running with active timer
acceptance_criteria: CD pipeline deploys latest main branch to production Function App without errors.
Function App timer trigger active and next run scheduled.
README.md includes: project description, architecture diagram or description, TRAPI dependency, blob output format, how to trigger manually, alert runbook.
All spec.md success criteria checkboxes marked complete.
Monitoring alert confirmed active in Azure Monitor.
Zero critical security findings in final gitleaks scan.
risks: Production deployment may require additional approval gates not defined in spec.
TRAPI dependency is external — outage on first nightly run would produce empty blob.
